quoteLast: of the following evening the submarine fleet received the word to be off.
title: The Brighton Boys with the Submarine Fleet
author: Driscoll, James R.

quoteFirst: Magnus's to Evening Shade; and I started
quoteLast: (when the moon rose), with twenty men, to go round that way, directing the remainder of the rear-guard to follow the main body at midnight.
title: Whip and Spur
author: Waring, George E. (George Edwin), 1833-1898

quoteFirst: Ann for her to lie in now she hath her fits of the ague.  Thence I went to Will's and staid like a fool there and played at cards till
quoteLast: and so came home, where I found Mr. Hunt and his wife who staid and sat with me till 10 and so good night.
title: Diary of Samuel Pepys — Volume 03: February 1659-1660
author: Pepys, Samuel, 1633-1703; Braybrooke, Richard Griffin, Baron, 1783-1858 [Editor]; Wheatley, Henry B. (Henry Benjamin), 1838-1917 [Editor]; Bright, Mynors, 1818-1883 [Translator]

quoteFirst: Towards the evening I had driven a wounded man to the field hospital near the market-place. The field hospital was established in a monastery. About
quoteLast: I drove the car with Captain von Harnier in it from the monastery back to the market-place, when suddenly firing began on all sides from the houses. I stopped my car and remained unhurt; Captain von Harnier was wounded in the arm; he hurried to the market-place, and I sought cover beneath the car.
title: The German Army in Belgium, the White Book of May 1915
author: Germany. Auswärtiges Amt; Bennett, Ernest Nathaniel, 1865-1947 [Translator]

quoteFirst: I think I will let events take their course. If we are to meet again, why, we are. If not, why, all our poor efforts can not compass it. Ah, it is
quoteLast: , on the very stroke! Good night." She smiled graciously, charmingly. "And thank you again for so kindly restoring my property.
title: The Silver Butterfly
author: Woodrow, Wilson, Mrs., 1870?-1935; Christy, Howard Chandler, 1873-1952 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: Before sunset, the temporary batteries of the enemy were all silenced, and every attempt to cross from Plattsburg to the American works repelled.
quoteLast: , the object was abandoned, and the British general hastily drew off his forces... Large quantities of military stores were abandoned, and fell into the hands of the Americans.
title:

quoteLast: quiet again reigned. Our first line was left entirely in the enemy's hands, but the second line after being lost and recaptured, then lost again and again retaken, remained wholly in our possession....

quoteFirst: And the gouty old gentleman, leaning on his cane, went to walk in the little garden till his guest should awake.
quoteLast: supper was served, for Mathias took supper. The old man was not a little astonished, when Paul joined him, to see that his old client’s brow was calm and his face serene, though noticeably changed.
title: The Marriage Contract
author: Balzac, Honoré de, 1799-1850; Wormeley, Katharine Prescott, 1830-1908 [Translator]

quoteFirst: Arthur, she said, had been depressed for several days, and at dinner had hardly spoken at all. He had not, however, objected to the dance. He had, indeed, seemed strangely determined to go, although she had pleaded a headache.
quoteLast: he went upstairs, apparently to dress.
title: Sight Unseen
author: Rinehart, Mary Roberts, 1876-1958

quoteFirst: He kept a clock in his bedroom, and rose in the early dawn. During the day he usually took a gallop, and in the twilight walked to the top of Monticello.
quoteLast: in summer and ten in winter were his hours for retiring. At a very early period he introduced a minute and exact system into all his affairs.
